sourceMovie.title: One Cut of the Dead
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: One Cut of the Dead works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Fido
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If One Cut of the Dead clicked for you, Fido (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after One Cut of the Dead?
faq.answer: Try Fido, What We Do in the Shadows, The Return of the Living Dead—each shares DNA with One Cut of the Dead while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is One Cut of the Dead?
faq.answer: One Cut of the Dead (2017) is best described as Comedy, Horror, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is One Cut of the Dead part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "One Cut of the Dead Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
